title: Fox Sports

infobox.value: * [Fox Corporation](/wiki/Fox_Corporation "Fox Corporation") (United States, Canada and Mexico) * [Foxtel](/wiki/Foxtel "Foxtel") (Australia) * [The Walt Disney Company](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") (Latin America, except Mexico and Argentina) * [Mediapro](/wiki/Mediapro "Mediapro") (Argentina)

* [Fox Sports United States](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(United_States) "Fox Sports (United States)") * [Fox Sports Mexico](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Mexican_TV_network) "Fox Sports (Mexican TV network)") * [Fox Sports Argentina](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Argentina) "Fox Sports (Argentina)") * [Fox Sports Australia](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Australia) "Fox Sports (Australia)")

is the brand name for a number of sports channels, broadcast divisions, programming, and other media around the world. The name originates from [Fox Broadcasting Company](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") in the United States, which in turn derives its name from [Fox Film](/wiki/Fox_Film "Fox Film") (which merged with [Twentieth Century Pictures](/wiki/Twentieth_Century_Pictures "Twentieth Century Pictures") to form [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox") in 1935), named after founder [William Fox](/wiki/William_Fox_(producer) "William Fox (producer)").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American brand of television programming

This article is about the international Fox Sports brand. For the U.S.-based division, see [Fox Sports (United States)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(United_States) "Fox Sports (United States)").

Fox Sports

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Product type | Trademark |
| Owner | * [Fox Corporation](/wiki/Fox_Corporation "Fox Corporation") (United States, Canada and Mexico) * [Foxtel](/wiki/Foxtel "Foxtel") (Australia) * [The Walt Disney Company](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") (Latin America, except Mexico and Argentina) * [Mediapro](/wiki/Mediapro "Mediapro") (Argentina) |
| Produced by | [Rupert Murdoch](/wiki/Rupert_Murdoch "Rupert Murdoch") |
| Country | [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States") |
| Introduced | August 12, 1994; 31 years ago (1994-08-12) |
| Related brands | * [Fox Sports United States](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(United_States) "Fox Sports (United States)") * [Fox Sports Mexico](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Mexican_TV_network) "Fox Sports (Mexican TV network)") * [Fox Sports Argentina](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Argentina) "Fox Sports (Argentina)") * [Fox Sports Australia](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Australia) "Fox Sports (Australia)") |
| Markets | Worldwide |
| Previous owners | * [News Corporation](/wiki/News_Corporation_(1980%E2%80%932013) "News Corporation (1980–2013)") * [21st Century Fox](/wiki/21st_Century_Fox "21st Century Fox") |
| Website | [www.foxsports.com](http://www.foxsports.com) |

**Fox Sports** is the brand name for a number of sports channels, broadcast divisions, programming, and other media around the world. The name originates from [Fox Broadcasting Company](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") in the United States, which in turn derives its name from [Fox Film](/wiki/Fox_Film "Fox Film") (which merged with [Twentieth Century Pictures](/wiki/Twentieth_Century_Pictures "Twentieth Century Pictures") to form [20th Century Fox](/wiki/20th_Century_Fox "20th Century Fox") in 1935), named after founder [William Fox](/wiki/William_Fox_(producer) "William Fox (producer)").

The *Fox Sports* name has since been used for other sports media assets. These assets are held mainly by [Fox Corporation](/wiki/Fox_Corporation "Fox Corporation"), with the exceptions of the [operations in Australia](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Australia) "Fox Sports (Australia)") (which are part of [Foxtel](/wiki/Foxtel "Foxtel"), entirely owned by [DAZN](/wiki/DAZN "DAZN") as of April 2025), [Mexico](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Mexico "Fox Sports Mexico") (owned by Grupo Multimedia Lauman), [Argentina](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Argentina) "Fox Sports (Argentina)") (owned by [Mediapro](/wiki/Mediapro "Mediapro") but branding and contents are licensed to Fox Corporation[[1]](#cite_note-FSArg-1)), and the rest of [Fox Sports International](/wiki/Fox_Sports_International "Fox Sports International") that was sold to [the Walt Disney Company](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") in Latin America, except Mexico and Argentina.[[2]](#cite_note-Fox_Sports_and_Foxtel_merger-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-nyt-4)

## Divisions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Divisions")]

* [Fox Sports (United States)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(United_States) "Fox Sports (United States)"), also known as the Fox Sports Media Group.
  + [FoxSports.com](/wiki/FoxSports.com "FoxSports.com"), a sports news website.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
  + [Fox Sports Radio](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Radio "Fox Sports Radio") is a national sports talk radio network managed by Premiere Networks in partnership with Fox Sports.
* [Fox Sports Australia](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Australia "Fox Sports Australia"), formerly Premier Media Group, owned by [Foxtel](/wiki/Foxtel "Foxtel") (A [DAZN](/wiki/DAZN "DAZN") Company).[[6]](#cite_note-News_Corp._Acquires_Consolidated_Media_Holdings-6)
* [Fox Sports Argentina](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Argentina) "Fox Sports (Argentina)"), owned by [Mediapro](/wiki/Mediapro "Mediapro"), branding licensed from [Fox Corporation](/wiki/Fox_Corporation "Fox Corporation")[[1]](#cite_note-FSArg-1)[[7]](#cite_note-7)
* [Fox Sports Mexico](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Mexico "Fox Sports Mexico"), owned by Grupo Multimedia Lauman, brand licensing currently under legal dispute between Lauman and [Fox Corporation](/wiki/Fox_Corporation "Fox Corporation").[[8]](#cite_note-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

## Channels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Channels")]

### Current channels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Current channels")]

#### Australia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Australia")]

* [Fox Sports (Australia)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Australia) "Fox Sports (Australia)"), a group of sports channels
  + [Fox Sports News](/wiki/Fox_Sports_News_(Australia) "Fox Sports News (Australia)") 500 – Cable and satellite channel that continuously televises sports news 24 hours per day.
  + [Fox Cricket](/wiki/Fox_Cricket "Fox Cricket") 501 – A 24-hour dedicated [Cricket](/wiki/Cricket "Cricket") channel
  + [Fox League](/wiki/Fox_League "Fox League") 502 – A 24-hour dedicated [Rugby League](/wiki/Rugby_League "Rugby League") channel
  + Fox Sports 503
  + [Fox Footy](/wiki/Fox_Footy "Fox Footy") 504 – A 24-hour dedicated [Australian Football League](/wiki/Australian_Football_League "Australian Football League") channel
  + Fox Sports 505
  + Fox Sports 506
  + Fox Sports More+ 507

#### Argentina

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Argentina")]

* [Fox Sports (Argentina)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Argentina) "Fox Sports (Argentina)"), is an Argentine pay television network that broadcasts in [Argentina](/wiki/Argentina "Argentina").
  + Fox Sports: formerly known as Prime Deportiva and Fox Sports Américas.
  + Fox Sports 2: formerly known as Fox Sports+
  + Fox Sports 3: formerly known as Speed Channel, it was launched in 2012 and its programming is car-related.

#### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Canada")]

* [Fox Sports Racing](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Racing "Fox Sports Racing"): Formerly known as the [Speed](/wiki/Speed_(TV_network) "Speed (TV network)") Channel, it was launched in 2013 and primarily simulcasts motorsports content mostly from [Fox Sports 1](/wiki/Fox_Sports_1 "Fox Sports 1") and [Fox Sports 2](/wiki/Fox_Sports_2 "Fox Sports 2")

#### Mexico

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Mexico")]

* [Fox Sports (Mexico)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Mexico) "Fox Sports (Mexico)"), is a Mexican pay television network that broadcasts in [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico").
  + Fox Sports: formerly known as Prime Deportiva and Fox Sports Américas.
  + Fox Sports 2: formerly known as Fox Sports+
  + Fox Sports 3: formerly known as Speed Channel, it was launched in 2012 and its programming is car-related.
  + Fox Sports Premium, is a pay-TV channel launched in 2022, it is specialised in broadcasting Mexican First Football Division matches.

#### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: United States")]

* [Fox Sports 1](/wiki/Fox_Sports_1 "Fox Sports 1") is a national sports network.
* [Fox Sports 2](/wiki/Fox_Sports_2 "Fox Sports 2") is a national sports network.
* [Fox Deportes](/wiki/Fox_Deportes "Fox Deportes") presents sports programming in Spanish.
* [Fox Soccer Plus](/wiki/Fox_Soccer_Plus "Fox Soccer Plus"), Originally a spin-off of [Fox Soccer](/wiki/Fox_Soccer "Fox Soccer"), with extended coverage of soccer and other international sport events.

### Former channels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Former channels")]

#### Africa

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Africa")]

* [Fox Sports Africa](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Africa "Fox Sports Africa"), launched in August 2014 in Sub-Saharan Africa.

#### Asia

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=11 "Edit section: Asia")]

* [Fox Sports](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Southeast_Asian_TV_network) "Fox Sports (Southeast Asian TV network)"), was a group of sports channels available in East and Southeast Asia, formerly ESPN Star Sports. These channels were closed on October 1, 2021.
  + Fox Sports, formerly ESPN.
  + Fox Sports 2, formerly Star Sports.
  + Fox Sports 3, formerly ESPN HD and Fox Sports Plus HD.

#### Brazil

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=12 "Edit section: Brazil")]

* [Fox Sports Brazil](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Brazil) "Fox Sports (Brazil)"), It was the Brazilian division of Fox Sports, that included 2 channels aimed at broadcasting sporting events 24 hours a day. Its main channel was rebranded on January 17, 2022, as [ESPN4](/wiki/ESPN4 "ESPN4"):
  + Fox Sports: since February 5, 2012, the channel was already available in all Brazil. On January 17, 2022, Fox Sports was renamed ESPN 4.[[10]](#cite_note-10)
  + [Fox Sports 2](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Brazil) "Fox Sports (Brazil)"): launched on January 24, 2014. Now run by Disney; remained under that name, due to contractual language with [CONMEBOL](/wiki/CONMEBOL "CONMEBOL") regarding its coverage of the [2022 Copa Libertadores](/wiki/2022_Copa_Libertadores "2022 Copa Libertadores").[[11]](#cite_note-ESPN4-11) On 15 February 2024, Fox Sports 2 was renamed ESPN 5.[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)

#### Canada

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=13 "Edit section: Canada")]

* [Fox Sports World Canada](/wiki/Fox_Sports_World_Canada "Fox Sports World Canada"), a defunct [specialty channel](/wiki/Specialty_channel "Specialty channel") primarily featuring [soccer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") and other events around the world that operated from 2001 to 2012.

#### Latin America

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=14 "Edit section: Latin America")]

* [Fox Sports](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Latin_American_TV_network) "Fox Sports (Latin American TV network)"), was a group of channels broadcast across Mexico, Central, and South America. Its main channel was rebranded on December 1, 2021, as [ESPN4](/wiki/ESPN_(Latin_America) "ESPN (Latin America)"):
  + Fox Sports: formerly known as Prime Deportiva and Fox Sports Américas. On 1 December 2021, Fox Sports was renamed ESPN 4.[[14]](#cite_note-14)
  + Fox Sports 1 (Chile): formerly known as Fox Sports Premium. On 15 February 2024, Fox Sports 1 was renamed ESPN Premium.[[15]](#cite_note-15)
  + Fox Sports 2: formerly known as Fox Sports+. On 15 February 2024, Fox Sports 2 was renamed ESPN 7.[[16]](#cite_note-16)
  + Fox Sports 3 (not available in Brazil): formerly known as Speed Channel, it was launched in 2012. On 15 February 2024, Fox Sports 3 was renamed ESPN 6.[[17]](#cite_note-17)
  + Fox Sports Premium (Argentina): A pay-TV channel launched in 2017, it specialises in broadcasting Argentine First Football Division matches. On May 1, 2022, the channel was renamed ESPN Premium.[[18]](#cite_note-18)

#### Middle East

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=15 "Edit section: Middle East")]

* [Fox Sports Middle East](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Middle_Eastern_TV_channel) "Fox Sports (Middle Eastern TV channel)") was a sports channel that was distributed by [Star Select](/w/index.php?title=Star_Select&action=edit&redlink=1 "Star Select (page does not exist)") as part of package television channels.

#### Netherlands

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=16 "Edit section: Netherlands")]

* [Fox Sports (Netherlands)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_(Netherlands) "Fox Sports (Netherlands)")[[19]](#cite_note-19) was a group of sports channels owned by Eredivisie Media & Marketing which for 51% was owned by [Fox Networks Group Benelux](/wiki/Fox_Networks_Group_Benelux "Fox Networks Group Benelux"), the channels was rebranded as ESPN.
  + [Fox Sports Eredivisie](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Eredivisie "Fox Sports Eredivisie"), 3 premium channels. It held the exclusive rights for the live matches of the [Eredivisie](/wiki/Eredivisie "Eredivisie"), the highest [Dutch](/wiki/Netherlands "Netherlands") football division.
  + [Fox Sports International (Netherlands)](/wiki/Fox_Sports_International_(Netherlands) "Fox Sports International (Netherlands)"), 3 premium channels covered several European football leagues.

#### Israel

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=17 "Edit section: Israel")]

* [Fox Sports Israel](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Israel "Fox Sports Israel") was first broadcast in [Israel](/wiki/Israel "Israel") back in 2001 by satellite provider [yes](/wiki/Yes_(Israel) "Yes (Israel)") and since 2010 it broadcasts the HD version of the channel.

#### Italy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=18 "Edit section: Italy")]

* [Fox Sports Italy](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Italy "Fox Sports Italy") was an Italian sports channel launched in 2013 alongside Fox Sports Plus and Fox Sports 2, which carries soccer, MLB, NFL, NCAA Sports, Volley Champions League and [Euroleague Basketball](/wiki/Euroleague_Basketball "Euroleague Basketball"). In 2018, the channel has been dissolved.

#### Turkey

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Turkey")]

* [Fox Sports Turkey](/wiki/Fox_Sports_T%C3%BCrkiye "Fox Sports Türkiye") was a sports channel that was distributed by the Turkish provider [Digiturk](/wiki/Digiturk "Digiturk").

#### South Korea

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=20 "Edit section: South Korea")]

* [JTBC3 Fox Sports](/wiki/JTBC_Sports "JTBC Sports"), a sports channel was owned by joint owned by [Fox Networks Group Asia Pacific](/wiki/Fox_Networks_Group_Asia_Pacific "Fox Networks Group Asia Pacific") and [JTBC](/wiki/JTBC "JTBC").

#### Japan

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=21 "Edit section: Japan")]

* [Fox Sports & Entertainment](/wiki/Fox_Sports_%26_Entertainment "Fox Sports & Entertainment") began producing sports shows in 2013, which are broadcast in several Fox channels. The channel closed on March 31, 2020.

#### United States

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=22 "Edit section: United States")]

* [Fox College Sports](/wiki/Fox_College_Sports "Fox College Sports") was a suite of three region-specific networks devoted to college sports that were sold to [Diamond Sports Group](/wiki/Diamond_Sports_Group "Diamond Sports Group") in 2019.
* [Fox Soccer](/wiki/Fox_Soccer "Fox Soccer")
* [Fox Sports Networks](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Networks "Fox Sports Networks") was a collection of [cable TV](/wiki/Cable_television "Cable television") [regional sports networks](/wiki/Regional_sports_network "Regional sports network") broadcasting across the United States that was sold to [Diamond Sports Group](/wiki/Diamond_Sports_Group "Diamond Sports Group") in 2019. In 2021, these networks were relaunched under the [Bally Sports](/wiki/Bally_Sports "Bally Sports") branding. These channels include:
  + [Fox Sports Arizona](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Arizona "Fox Sports Arizona")
  + [Fox Sports Carolinas](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Carolinas "Fox Sports Carolinas")
  + [Fox Sports Detroit](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Detroit "Fox Sports Detroit")
  + [Fox Sports Florida](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Florida "Fox Sports Florida")
  + [Fox Sports Indiana](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Indiana "Fox Sports Indiana")
  + [Fox Sports Kansas City](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Kansas_City "Fox Sports Kansas City")
  + [Fox Sports Midwest](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Midwest "Fox Sports Midwest")
  + [Fox Sports New Orleans](/wiki/Fox_Sports_New_Orleans "Fox Sports New Orleans")
  + [Fox Sports North](/wiki/Fox_Sports_North "Fox Sports North")
  + [Fox Sports Ohio](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Ohio "Fox Sports Ohio")
  + [Fox Sports Oklahoma](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Oklahoma "Fox Sports Oklahoma")
  + [Fox Sports San Diego](/wiki/Fox_Sports_San_Diego "Fox Sports San Diego")
  + [Fox Sports South](/wiki/Fox_Sports_South "Fox Sports South")
  + [Fox Sports Southeast](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Southeast "Fox Sports Southeast")
  + [Fox Sports Southwest](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Southwest "Fox Sports Southwest")
  + [Fox Sports Sun](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Sun "Fox Sports Sun")
  + [Fox Sports Tennessee](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Tennessee "Fox Sports Tennessee")
  + [Fox Sports West](/wiki/Fox_Sports_West "Fox Sports West")
  + [Fox Sports Wisconsin](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Wisconsin "Fox Sports Wisconsin")
  + [Fox Sports Northwest](/wiki/Root_Sports_Northwest "Root Sports Northwest")
  + [Fox Sports Pittsburgh](/wiki/Root_Sports_Pittsburgh "Root Sports Pittsburgh")
  + [Fox Sports Rocky Mountain](/wiki/Root_Sports_Rocky_Mountain "Root Sports Rocky Mountain")
  + [Fox Sports Utah](/wiki/Fox_Sports_Utah "Fox Sports Utah")
  + [Prime Ticket](/wiki/Bally_Sports_West "Bally Sports West")
  + [SportsTime Ohio](/wiki/SportsTime_Ohio "SportsTime Ohio")
  + [YES Network](/wiki/YES_Network "YES Network")

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Fox_Sports&action=edit&section=24 "Edit section: References")]
